pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the unified release flag for Java 9+ module system compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: Using source/target with Java 21 may cause warnings or incompatibilities with module system features; release is the standard for cross-compilation in modern Java.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add release flag to test compiler configuration to ensure tests are compiled against the target Java version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without the release flag, tests might be compiled with default settings that don't match the target runtime, potentially causing runtime errors or missing API availability.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct semantic marking of serialization metadata.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is not available without importing java.io.Serial, causing a compile error.
